</div>

<article class="level2 chunking-large type-Chapter chunknode" id="15" data-cite="Chapter 15 KZC" data-short-cite="Ch. 15"><header class=" mini-header" data-type="Chapter" data-search="/KZC/15/search" data-share="/KZC/15/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h2 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">Chapter 15</span> <span class="name">LOW DENSITY RESIDENTIAL ZONES</span></h2><p class="subname">(RS, RSX, RSA, WD II, PLA 3C, PLA 6E, PLA 16)</p></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This chapter is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="tocHeading heading">Sections:</p><ul class="toc from-level2 to-level6">

<li class="tocItem level4"><a href="15.05"><span class="num">15.05</span> <span class="name">User Guide.</span></a><ul><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.05.010"><span class="num">15.05.010</span> <span class="name">Applicable Zones.</span></a></li><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.05.020"><span class="num">15.05.020</span> <span class="name">Common Code References.</span></a></li></ul></li>
<li class="tocItem level4"><a href="15.10"><span class="num">15.10</span> <span class="name">General Regulations.</span></a><ul><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.10.010"><span class="num">15.10.010</span> <span class="name">All Low Density Residential Zones.</span></a></li><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.10.020"><span class="num">15.10.020</span> <span class="name">RS Zone.</span></a></li><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.10.030"><span class="num">15.10.030</span> <span class="name">RSA Zone.</span></a></li><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.10.040"><span class="num">15.10.040</span> <span class="name">WD II Zone.</span></a></li><li class="tocItem level6"><a href="15.10.050"><span class="num">15.10.050</span> <span class="name">PLA 3C Zone.</span></a></li></ul></li>
<li class="tocItem level4"><a href="15.20"><span class="num">15.20</span> <span class="name">Permitted Uses.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level4"><a href="15.30"><span class="num">15.30</span> <span class="name">Density/Dimensions.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level4"><a href="15.40"><span class="num">15.40</span> <span class="name">Development Standards.</span></a></li>
</ul>
<article class="level4 chunking-small type-Section" id="15.05" data-cite="KZC 15.05" data-short-cite="15.05"><header class=" mini-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.05/search" data-share="/KZC/15.05/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h4 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.05&#x2002;</span><span class="name">User Guide.</span></h4></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-StepSubsec1" id="15.05-Step-1" data-cite="KZC 15.05, Step 1"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">Step 1.&#x2002;</span></span>Check that the zone of interest is included in KZC <a class="cite" href="15.05.010" title="15.05.010&#x2002;Applicable Zones">15.05.010</a>, Applicable Zones. If not, select the chapter where it is located.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-StepSubsec1" id="15.05-Step-2" data-cite="KZC 15.05, Step 2"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">Step 2.&#x2002;</span></span>Refer to KZC <a class="cite" href="15.05.020" title="15.05.020&#x2002;Common Code References">15.05.020</a>, Common Code References, for relevant information found elsewhere in the code.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-StepSubsec1" id="15.05-Step-3" data-cite="KZC 15.05, Step 3"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">Step 3.&#x2002;</span></span>Refer to the General Regulations in KZC <a class="cite" href="15.10" title="15.10&#x2002;General Regulations">15.10</a> that apply to the zones as noted.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-StepSubsec1" id="15.05-Step-4" data-cite="KZC 15.05, Step 4"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">Step 4.&#x2002;</span></span>Find the Use of interest in the Permitted Uses Table in KZC <a class="cite" href="15.20" title="15.20&#x2002;Permitted Uses">15.20</a> and read across to the column pertaining to the zone of interest. If a Use is not listed in the table, it is not allowed. A listed use is permitted unless &#x201C;NP&#x201D; (Not Permitted) is noted for the table. Note the Required Review Process and Special Regulations that are applicable. There are links to the Special Regulations listed immediately following the table (PU-1, PU-2, PU-3, etc.).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-StepSubsec1" id="15.05-Step-5" data-cite="KZC 15.05, Step 5"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">Step 5.&#x2002;</span></span>Find the Use of interest in the Density/Dimensions Table in KZC <a class="cite" href="15.30" title="15.30&#x2002;Density/Dimensions">15.30</a> and read across the columns. Note the standards (Minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">Lot Size</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.775">Required Yards</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.510">Maximum Lot Coverage</a>, and Maximum <a class="def" href="5.10.357">Height of Structure</a>) and Special Regulations that are applicable. There are links to the Special Regulations listed immediately following the table (DD-1, DD-2, DD-3, etc.).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-StepSubsec1" id="15.05-Step-6" data-cite="KZC 15.05, Step 6"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">Step 6.&#x2002;</span></span>Find the Use of interest in the Development Standards Table in KZC <a class="cite" href="15.40" title="15.40&#x2002;Development Standards">15.40</a> and read across the columns. Note the standards (<a class="def" href="5.10.465">Landscape</a> Category, <a class="def" href="5.10.845">Sign</a> Category, and Required <a class="def" href="5.10.635">Parking Spaces</a>) and Special Regulations that are applicable. There are links to the Special Regulations listed immediately following the table (DS-1, DS-2, DS-3, etc.).</p>



</section>
<p><i>Note: Not all uses listed in the Density/Dimensions and Development Standards Tables are permitted in each zone addressed in this chapter. Permitted uses are determined only by the Permitted Uses Table.</i></p>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection" id="15.05.010" data-cite="KZC 15.05.010" data-short-cite="15.05.010"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.05.010/search" data-share="/KZC/15.05.010/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.05.010&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Applicable Zones.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>This chapter contains the regulations for uses in the following zones of the City: RS 35, RS 12.5, RS 8.5, RS 7.2, RS 6.3 and RS 5.0; RSX 35, RSX 12.5, RSX 8.5, RSX 7.2 and RSX 5.0 zones; RSA 1, RSA 4, RSA 6 and RSA 8 zones; WD II zones; PLA 3C, PLA 6E, and PLA 16 zones.</p>
</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection has-history" id="15.05.020" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020" data-short-cite="15.05.020" data-history="/KZC/15.05.020/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.05.020/search" data-share="/KZC/15.05.020/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.05.020&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Common Code References.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(1)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(1)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Refer to Chapter <a class="cite" href="1" title="Ch. 1&#x2002;User Guide">1</a> KZC to determine what other provisions of this code may apply to the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(2)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(2)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.730">Public park</a> development standards will be determined on a case-by-case basis. See KZC <a class="cite" href="45.50" title="45.50&#x2002;Public Park">45.50</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(3)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(3)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>For properties within the Holmes Point (HP) Overlay Zone, see Chapter <a class="cite" href="70" title="Ch. 70&#x2002;Holmes Point Overlay Zone">70</a> KZC for additional regulations.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(4)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(4)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Review processes, density/dimensions and development standards for shoreline uses (RS, RSA, WD II, PLA 3C zones) can be found in Chapter <a class="cite" href="83" title="Ch. 83&#x2002;Shoreline Management">83</a> KZC, Shoreline Management.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(5)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(5)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Chapter <a class="cite" href="115" title="Ch. 115&#x2002;Miscellaneous Use Development and Performance Standards">115</a> KZC contains regulations regarding <a class="def" href="5.10.370">home occupations</a> and other <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessory</a> uses, facilities and activities associated with <a class="def" href="5.10.255">Attached Dwelling Units</a> in PLA 3C and <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a> uses.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(6)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(6)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>A <a class="def" href="5.10.347">hazardous liquid pipeline</a> is located near the RSX 35 zone in the Bridle Trails neighborhood along the eastern boundary of the City, and extends through or near the RSA 1, 4, 6 and 8 zones in the vicinity of 136th Avenue NE. Refer to Chapter <a class="cite" href="118" title="Ch. 118&#x2002;Hazardous Liquid Pipelines">118</a> KZC for regulations pertaining to properties near <a class="def" href="5.10.347">hazardous liquid pipelines</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(7)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(7)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>Garages shall comply with the requirements of KZC <a class="cite" href="115.43" title="115.43&#x2002;Garage Requirements for Detached Dwelling Units in Low Density Zones">115.43</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(8)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(8)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(8)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">8.&#x2002;</span></span>For properties within the Goat Hill (GH) Overlay Zone, see Chapter <a class="cite" href="71" title="Ch. 71&#x2002;Goat Hill Overlay Zone">71</a> KZC for additional regulations.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.05.020(9)" data-cite="KZC 15.05.020(9)" data-short-cite="15.05.020(9)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">9.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.524">Middle housing</a> is permitted as an alternative to the <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a> use; see Chapter <a class="cite" href="113" title="Ch. 113&#x2002;Middle Housing">113</a> KZC for additional regulations. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 4905 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2025; Ord. 4879 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2024; Ord. 4811 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2022; Ord. 4506 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2015; Ord. 4476 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2015)</span></p>



</section>







</article>
</article>
<article class="level4 chunking-small type-Section" id="15.10" data-cite="KZC 15.10" data-short-cite="15.10"><header class=" mini-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.10/search" data-share="/KZC/15.10/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h4 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.10&#x2002;</span><span class="name">General Regulations.</span></h4></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection" id="15.10.010" data-cite="KZC 15.10.010" data-short-cite="15.10.010"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.10.010/search" data-share="/KZC/15.10.010/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.10.010&#x2002;</span><span class="name">All Low Density Residential Zones.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.010(1)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.010(1)" data-short-cite="15.10.010(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.910">Structures</a> located within 30 feet of a parcel in a <a class="def" href="5.10.490">low density zone</a> or a <a class="def" href="5.10.485">low density use</a> in PLA 17 shall comply with additional limitations on <a class="def" href="5.10.910">structure</a> size established by KZC <a class="cite" href="115.136" title="115.136&#x2002;Size Limitations for Structures Abutting or Within Low Density Zones and Abutting Low Density Uses in PLA 17">115.136</a>, except for the following uses: KZC <a class="cite" href="15.20" title="15.20&#x2002;Permitted Uses">15.20.060</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a>, and KZC <a class="cite" href="15.20" title="15.20&#x2002;Permitted Uses">15.20.100</a>, Piers, Docks, Boat Lifts and Canopies Serving <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a>.</p>



</section>
</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection" id="15.10.020" data-cite="KZC 15.10.020" data-short-cite="15.10.020"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.10.020/search" data-share="/KZC/15.10.020/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.10.020&#x2002;</span><span class="name">RS Zone.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.020(1)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.020(1)" data-short-cite="15.10.020(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>May not use lands <a class="def" href="5.10.980">waterward</a> of the <a class="def" href="5.10.611">ordinary high water mark</a> to determine <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> or to calculate allowable density.</p>



</section>
</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection" id="15.10.030" data-cite="KZC 15.10.030" data-short-cite="15.10.030"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.10.030/search" data-share="/KZC/15.10.030/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.10.030&#x2002;</span><span class="name">RSA Zone.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.030(1)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.030(1)" data-short-cite="15.10.030(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>All subdivisions and short subdivisions in the RSA 1 zone shall be clustered such that development is located away from <a class="def" href="5.10.179.5">critical areas</a>. The <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> resulting from such clustering shall be placed in a separate tract that includes at least 50 percent of the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a>. <a class="def" href="5.10.610">Open space</a> tracts shall be permanent and shall be dedicated to a homeowner&#x2019;s association or other suitable organization for purposes of maintenance. Passive recreation, with no development of <a class="def" href="5.10.279">recreational facilities</a>, and natural-surface pedestrian and <a class="def" href="5.10.285">equestrian</a> trails are acceptable uses within the <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> tract. If access to the <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> is provided, the access shall be located in a separate tract. A greenbelt protection or <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> <a class="def" href="5.10.270">easement</a> shall be dedicated to the City to protect the designated <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> tract resulting from <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> clustering.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.030(2)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.030(2)" data-short-cite="15.10.030(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>May not use lands <a class="def" href="5.10.980">waterward</a> of the <a class="def" href="5.10.611">ordinary high water mark</a> to determine <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> or to calculate allowable density.</p>



</section>
</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection" id="15.10.040" data-cite="KZC 15.10.040" data-short-cite="15.10.040"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.10.040/search" data-share="/KZC/15.10.040/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.10.040&#x2002;</span><span class="name">WD II Zone.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.040(1)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.040(1)" data-short-cite="15.10.040(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>May not use lands <a class="def" href="5.10.980">waterward</a> of the <a class="def" href="5.10.611">ordinary high water mark</a> to determine <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> or to calculate allowable density.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.040(2)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.040(2)" data-short-cite="15.10.040(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>The <a class="def" href="5.10.775">required yard</a> abutting an unopened <a class="def" href="5.10.805">right-of-way</a> shall be a side property rather than a front <a class="def" href="5.10.720">property line</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.040(3)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.040(3)" data-short-cite="15.10.040(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>The required rear yard for each use shall be the same as the required rear yard for the same use in the RS zone, unless otherwise specified in KZC <a class="cite link-broken ctx-KMC" href="/KMC/30.25.010(9)">30.25.010(9)</a> and <a class="cite link-broken ctx-KMC" href="/KMC/30.25.010(10)">(10)</a>. (Does not apply to Piers, Docks, Boat Lifts and Canopies Serving <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a>.)</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.040(4)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.040(4)" data-short-cite="15.10.040(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.780">Residential uses</a> abutting Lake Washington may have an associated private shoreline park that is commonly owned and used by residents and guests.</p>



</section>
</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-FourPartSection has-history" id="15.10.050" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050" data-short-cite="15.10.050" data-history="/KZC/15.10.050/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.10.050/search" data-share="/KZC/15.10.050/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.10.050&#x2002;</span><span class="name">PLA 3C Zone.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="15.10.050(1)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050(1)" data-short-cite="15.10.050(1)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Development shall be subject to the following development standards:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="15.10.050(1)(a)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050(1)(a)" data-short-cite="15.10.050(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.910">Structures</a> must be clustered and located so that they will not significantly impact slope stability, drainage patterns, erosion or landslide hazards, and steep ravine areas on the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a> or adjacent property.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="15.10.050(1)(b)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050(1)(b)" data-short-cite="15.10.050(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Vegetative cover shall be retained to the maximum extent possible to stabilize slopes.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="15.10.050(1)(c)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050(1)(c)" data-short-cite="15.10.050(1)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Pursuant to the requirements of KZC 85.15(1) through 85.15(4), the <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> shall submit a geotechnical report prepared by a qualified geotechnical engineer evaluating the potential geologic hazard areas of the subject and adjacent properties to minimize damage to life and property. Specific structural designs and construction techniques to ensure long-term stability shall be considered as part of the analysis. The <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a>&#x2019;s geotechnical report and recommendations shall be reviewed by a qualified geotechnical engineer selected and retained by the City at the <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a>&#x2019;s expense. The <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> shall comply with the performance standards contained in KZC <a class="cite" href="85.25" title="85.25&#x2002;Performance Standards">85.25</a> and <a class="cite" href="85.45" title="85.45&#x2002;Liability">85.45</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="15.10.050(1)(d)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050(1)(d)" data-short-cite="15.10.050(1)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>The City may require <a class="def" href="5.10.930">traffic control devices</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.835">shared access points</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.810">right-of-way realignment</a>, or limit development if necessary to further reduce traffic impacts.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="15.10.050(1)(e)" data-cite="KZC 15.10.050(1)(e)" data-short-cite="15.10.050(1)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Development must ensure that the City has the ability to access and provide necessary emergency services. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 4476 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2015)</span></p>



</section>
</section>



</article>
</article>
<article class="level4 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="15.20" data-cite="KZC 15.20" data-short-cite="15.20" data-history="/KZC/15.20/history"><header class=" mini-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.20/search" data-share="/KZC/15.20/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h4 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.20&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Permitted Uses.</span></h4></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="PermittedUseTable role-large table-dense table-extra-dense table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Permitted Uses Table&#xA0;&#x2013; Low Density Residential Zones (RS, RSX, RSA, WD II, PLA 3C, PLA 6E, PLA 16)</span></p>


<p class="center"><b>(See also KZC <a class="cite" href="15.30" title="15.30&#x2002;Density/Dimensions">15.30</a>, Density/Dimensions Table, and KZC <a class="cite" href="15.40" title="15.40&#x2002;Development Standards">15.40</a>, Development Standards Table)</b></p>
</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 110pt;"/><col style="width: 110pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/><col style="width: 60pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="4" colspan="2">
<p><b>USE</b><br/>&#xA0;</p>
</th>
<th colspan="7">
<p class="heading"><b>Required Review Process: </b></p>
</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th colspan="4">
<p>I = Process I, Chapter <a class="cite" href="145" title="Ch. 145&#x2002;Process I">145</a> KZC</p>
<p>IIA = Process IIA, Chapter <a class="cite" href="150" title="Ch. 150&#x2002;Process IIA">150</a> KZC</p>
</th>
<th colspan="3">
<p>IIB = Process IIB, Chapter <a class="cite" href="152" title="Ch. 152&#x2002;Process IIB">152</a> KZC</p>
<p>None = No Required Review Process</p>
</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th class="center" colspan="7">
<p>NP = Use Not Permitted</p>
<p># = Applicable Special Regulations (listed after the table)</p>
</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>
<p><b>RS</b></p>
</th>
<th>

</th>

</tr>

<tr>
<td>
<p>15.20.110</p>

</td>
<td>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-2" title="PU-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-4b" title="PU-4b">4b</a></p>
</td>

</td>

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.20.130</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.824">School</a> or <a class="def" href="5.10.192">Day-Care Center</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-2" title="PU-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-4" title="PU-4">4</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-14" title="PU-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-16" title="PU-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-18" title="PU-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-20" title="PU-20">20</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-2" title="PU-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-4" title="PU-4">4</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-14" title="PU-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-16" title="PU-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-18" title="PU-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-20" title="PU-20">20</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-2" title="PU-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-4" title="PU-4">4</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-13" title="PU-13">13</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-14" title="PU-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-16" title="PU-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-18" title="PU-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-20" title="PU-20">20</a></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>NP</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>IIA</p>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-4" title="PU-4">4</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-14" title="PU-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-16" title="PU-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-18" title="PU-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-20" title="PU-20">20</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-2" title="PU-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-4" title="PU-4">4</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-14" title="PU-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-16" title="PU-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-18" title="PU-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-20" title="PU-20">20</a></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>IIA</p>
<p><a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-16" title="PU-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-17" title="PU-17">17</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-18" title="PU-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-19" title="PU-19">19</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.20-PU-20" title="PU-20">20</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<p class="heading"><b>Permitted Uses (PU) Special Regulations:</b></p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-1" data-cite="PU-1"><p class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-1.&#x2002;</span></p>


<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-1a" data-cite="PU-1a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>No more than two units may be attached to each other.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-1b" data-cite="PU-1b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.255">Attached Dwelling Units</a> must be designed to look like a detached single-<a class="def" href="5.10.300">family</a> house using such techniques as limiting the points of entry on each facade, providing pitched roofs and covered porches.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-2" data-cite="PU-2"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-2.&#x2002;</span></span>The required review process is as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-2a" data-cite="PU-2a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>If the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a>, including all contiguous property owned by the <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> and held by others for future use by the <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a>, is less than five acres, the required review process is Process IIA, Chapter <a class="cite" href="150" title="Ch. 150&#x2002;Process IIA">150</a> KZC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-2b" data-cite="PU-2b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>If the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a>, including all contiguous property owned by the <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> and held by others for future use by the <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a>, is five or more acres, a <a class="def" href="5.10.505">Master Plan</a>, approved through Process IIB, Chapter <a class="cite" href="152" title="Ch. 152&#x2002;Process IIB">152</a> KZC, is required. The <a class="def" href="5.10.505">Master Plan</a> must show <a class="def" href="5.10.090">building</a> placement, <a class="def" href="5.10.090">building</a> dimensions, roadways, utility locations, land uses within the <a class="def" href="5.10.505">Master Plan</a> area, parking location, buffering, and <a class="def" href="5.10.465">landscaping</a>.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-3" data-cite="PU-3"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-3.&#x2002;</span></span>Reserved.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-4" data-cite="PU-4"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-4.&#x2002;</span></span>May locate on the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a> only if:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-4a" data-cite="PU-4a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>It will not be materially detrimental to the character of the neighborhood in which it is located.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-4b" data-cite="PU-4b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Site and <a class="def" href="5.10.090">building</a> design minimizes adverse impacts on surrounding residential neighborhoods.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-4c" data-cite="PU-4c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>The property is served by a collector or arterial <a class="def" href="5.10.900">street</a> (does not apply to existing <a class="def" href="5.10.824">school</a> sites).</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-5" data-cite="PU-5"><p class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-5.&#x2002;</span></p>


<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-5a" data-cite="PU-5a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>This use may include arenas, stables, roaming and grazing areas, club house and ancillary <a class="def" href="5.10.285">equestrian</a> facilities.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-5b" data-cite="PU-5b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>This use must comply with KZC <a class="cite" href="80.30" title="80.30&#x2002;Effect&#xA0;&#x2013; Criteria for Conducting Equestrian Uses or Activities">80.30</a> through <a class="cite" href="80.45" title="80.45&#x2002;Effect&#xA0;&#x2013; Bonds">80.45</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-5c" data-cite="PU-5c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>An improved public <a class="def" href="5.10.285">equestrian</a> access trail through the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a> and appropriate public <a class="def" href="5.10.845">signing</a> must be provided. The trail must be located and designed to allow for an eventual connection between NE 60th <a class="def" href="5.10.900">Street</a> and Bridle Trails State and King County Parks.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-6" data-cite="PU-6"><p class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-6.&#x2002;</span></p>


<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-6a" data-cite="PU-6a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>This use may include activities such as: indoor and <a class="def" href="5.10.620">outdoor</a> tennis courts, club house, swimming pool, other sport court games and ancillary commercial recreation activities.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-6b" data-cite="PU-6b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Hours of operation may be limited by the City to reduce impacts on <a class="def" href="5.10.780">residential uses</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-6c" data-cite="PU-6c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Vehicular and pedestrian circulation to and from the property shall be coordinated with the other properties in the vicinity to the maximum extent possible.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-7" data-cite="PU-7"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-7.&#x2002;</span></span>If <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is less than 35,000 square feet, then Process IIB, Chapter <a class="cite" href="152" title="Ch. 152&#x2002;Process IIB">152</a> KZC.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-8" data-cite="PU-8"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-8.&#x2002;</span></span>For this use, only one <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> may be on each <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> regardless of the size of the <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a>, unless the <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> is being developed pursuant to the <a class="def" href="5.10.174">cottage</a>, carriage, or <a class="def" href="5.10.931.5">two/three-unit homes</a> regulations in Chapter <a class="cite" href="113" title="Ch. 113&#x2002;Middle Housing">113</a> KZC.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-9" data-cite="PU-9"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-9.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.780">Residential uses</a> abutting Lake Washington may have an associated private shoreline park that is commonly or individually owned and used by residents and guests.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-10" data-cite="PU-10"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-10.&#x2002;</span></span>See Chapter <a class="cite" href="141" title="Ch. 141&#x2002;Shoreline Administration">141</a> KZC for additional procedural requirements in addition to those in Chapter <a class="cite" href="145" title="Ch. 145&#x2002;Process I">145</a> KZC.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-11" data-cite="PU-11"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-11.&#x2002;</span></span>At the northern terminus of the 5th Avenue West <a class="def" href="5.10.973">vehicular access easement</a>, the <a class="def" href="5.10.060">average parcel depth</a> shall be measured from the <a class="def" href="5.10.611">ordinary high water mark</a> to the public pedestrian access <a class="def" href="5.10.270">easement</a> providing access to Waverly Beach Park.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-12" data-cite="PU-12"><p class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-12.&#x2002;</span></p>


<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-12a" data-cite="PU-12a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>May not include miniature golf.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-12b" data-cite="PU-12b"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>The following <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessory</a> uses are specifically permitted as part of this use:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-UseSubsec3" id="15.20-PU-12b1" data-cite="PU-12b1"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1)&#x2002;</span></span>Equipment storage facilities.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-UseSubsec3" id="15.20-PU-12b2" data-cite="PU-12b2"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2)&#x2002;</span></span>Retail sales and rental of golf equipment and <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessories</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-UseSubsec3" id="15.20-PU-12b3" data-cite="PU-12b3"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3)&#x2002;</span></span>A <a class="def" href="5.10.790">restaurant</a>.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-13" data-cite="PU-13"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-13.&#x2002;</span></span>This use is not permitted on properties within the jurisdiction of the <a class="def" href="5.10.837">Shoreline Management Act</a>.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-14" data-cite="PU-14"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-14.&#x2002;</span></span>Hours of operation and maximum number of attendees may be limited by the City to reduce impacts on nearby <a class="def" href="5.10.780">residential uses</a>.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-15" data-cite="PU-15"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-15.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.917">Structured play areas</a> must be set back from all <a class="def" href="5.10.720">property lines</a> by five feet.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-16" data-cite="PU-16"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-16.&#x2002;</span></span>May include <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessory</a> living facilities for staff <a class="def" href="5.10.650">persons</a>.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-17" data-cite="PU-17"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-17.&#x2002;</span></span>May locate on the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a> if:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-17a" data-cite="PU-17a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>It will serve the immediate neighborhood in which it is located; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-17b" data-cite="PU-17b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>It will not be materially detrimental to the character of the neighborhood in which it is located.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-18" data-cite="PU-18"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-18.&#x2002;</span></span>A six-foot-high <a class="def" href="5.10.310">fence</a> is required along the <a class="def" href="5.10.720">property lines</a> adjacent to the outside play areas.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-19" data-cite="PU-19"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-19.&#x2002;</span></span>Hours of operation may be limited by the City to reduce impacts on nearby <a class="def" href="5.10.780">residential uses</a>.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-20" data-cite="PU-20"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-20.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.917">Structured play areas</a> must be set back from all <a class="def" href="5.10.720">property lines</a> as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-20a" data-cite="PU-20a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Twenty feet if this use can accommodate 50 or more students or children.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.20-PU-20b" data-cite="PU-20b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Ten feet if this use can accommodate 13 to 49 students or children.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-21" data-cite="PU-21"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-21.&#x2002;</span></span>For properties zoned RSA 4 within the Goat Hill (GH) Overlay Zone, only one <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> is permitted on each <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> except for one attached <a class="def" href="5.10.017">Accessory Dwelling Unit</a>. See Chapter <a class="cite" href="71" title="Ch. 71&#x2002;Goat Hill Overlay Zone">71</a> KZC, Goat Hill Overlay Zone, for additional regulations.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-22" data-cite="PU-22"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-22.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.524">Middle housing</a> is permitted as an alternative to <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Units</a>; see Chapter <a class="cite" href="113" title="Ch. 113&#x2002;Middle Housing">113</a> KZC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-PUSubsec1" id="15.20-PU-23" data-cite="PU-23"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">PU-23.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.017">Accessory Dwelling Units</a> are permitted as an <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessory</a> use; see Chapter <a class="cite" href="115.07" title="115.07&#x2002;Accessory Dwelling Units">115.07</a> KZC. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 4913, 2025; Ord. 4905 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2025; Ord. 4879 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2024; Ord. 4811 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2022; Ord. 4749 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2021; Ord. 4506 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2015; Ord. 4476 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2015)</span></p>



</section>









</article>
<article class="level4 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="15.30" data-cite="KZC 15.30" data-short-cite="15.30" data-history="/KZC/15.30/history"><header class=" mini-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/KZC/15.30/search" data-share="/KZC/15.30/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h4 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">15.30&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Density/Dimensions.</span></h4></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="DensityDimensionTable role-large table-dense table-extra-dense table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Density/Dimensions Table&#xA0;&#x2013; Low Density Residential Zones (RS, RSX, RSA, WD II, PLA 3C, PLA 6E, PLA 16)</span>&#xA0;</p>


<p class="center"><b>(Refer to KZC <a class="cite" href="15.20" title="15.20&#x2002;Permitted Uses">15.20</a>, Permitted Uses Table, to determine if a use is allowed in the zone; see also KZC <a class="cite" href="15.40" title="15.40&#x2002;Development Standards">15.40</a>, Development Standards Table)</b></p>
</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 110pt;"/><col style="width: 50pt;"/><col style="width: 50pt;"/><col style="width: 50pt;"/><col style="width: 50pt;"/><col style="width: 150pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2" colspan="2">
<p><b>USE</b><br/>&#xA0;</p>
</th>
<th rowspan="2">
<p><b>Minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">Lot Size</a></b></p>
</th>
<th colspan="3">
<p><b><a class="def" href="5.10.775">REQUIRED YARDs</a></b><sup><a class="cite" href="1" title="Ch. 1&#x2002;User Guide">1</a></sup></p>
<p><b>(See Ch. 115 KZC)</b></p>
</th>
<th rowspan="2">
<p><b><a class="def" href="5.10.510">Maximum Lot Coverage</a></b></p>
</th>
<th rowspan="2">
<p><b>Maximum <a class="def" href="5.10.357">Height of Structure</a></b></p>
<p><b>ABE = <a class="def" href="5.10.045">Average Building Elevation</a></b></p>
</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>
<p><b>Front</b></p>
</th>
<th>
<p><b>Side </b></p>
</th>
<th>
<p><b>Rear</b></p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.010</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.255">Attached Dwelling Units</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-2" title="DD-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-3" title="DD-3">3</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-4" title="DD-4">4</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-5" title="DD-5">5</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-5" title="DD-5">5</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>25' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.020</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.299.6">Faith-Based Facility</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS, RSX, RSA: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-7" title="DD-7">7</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C:</b> 12,500 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 6E:</b> 7,200 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 16:</b> 35,000 sq. ft.</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>70%</p>
<p><b>RSA:</b> 70%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C:</b> 50%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS, PLA 3C, PLA 6E:</b> 25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSX, RSA, PLA 16:</b> 30' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.030</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>Commercial <a class="def" href="5.10.285">Equestrian</a> Facility</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>3 acres</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>80%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-8a" title="DD-8a">8a</a></sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.040</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.140">Commercial Recreation Area and Use</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>1 acre</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>80%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>38' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-8a" title="DD-8a">8a</a>, b</sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>15.30.050</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.153">Community Facility</a></p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>None</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'</p>
<p><b>WD II:</b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-9" title="DD-9">9</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'</p>
</td>
<td class="center" rowspan="2">
<p>70%</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>70%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>50%</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p><b>RS, WD II, PLA 3C, PLA 6E: </b>25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSA, RSX, PLA 16:</b> 30' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="3">
<p><b>WD II: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-10" title="DD-10">10</a></sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.060</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS, RSX: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-11" title="DD-11">11</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-12" title="DD-12">12</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-13" title="DD-13">13</a></sup></p>
<p><b>RSA: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-11" title="DD-11">11</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-17" title="DD-17">17</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-18" title="DD-18">18</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-19" title="DD-19">19</a></sup></p>
<p><b>WD II: </b>12,500 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>12,500 sq. ft.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-2" title="DD-2">2</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-27" title="DD-27">27</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 6E: </b>5,000 sq. ft.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-28" title="DD-28">28</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 16: </b>35,000 sq. ft.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-13" title="DD-13">13</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-29" title="DD-29">29</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-30" title="DD-30">30</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p><b>RS: </b>20'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-14" title="DD-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-16" title="DD-16">16</a></sup></p>
<p><b>RSX: </b>20'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-15" title="DD-15">15</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-16" title="DD-16">16</a></sup></p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>20'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-15" title="DD-15">15</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-16" title="DD-16">16</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-20" title="DD-20">20</a></sup></p>
<p><b>WD II: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-14" title="DD-14">14</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-22" title="DD-22">22</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-32" title="DD-32">32</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C, PLA 6E, PLA 16: </b>20'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-4" title="DD-4">4</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>5'/15'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-9" title="DD-9">9</a></sup></p>
<p><b>RSX: </b>5'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-15" title="DD-15">15</a></sup></p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>5'</p>
<p><b>WD II: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-23" title="DD-23">23</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-24" title="DD-24">24</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'</p>
<p><b>RS, RSX: </b>10'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-16" title="DD-16">16</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50%</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>50%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS, PLA 3C, PLA 6E: </b>25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSX, PLA 16: </b>30' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>30' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-21" title="DD-21">21</a></sup></p>
<p><b>WD II: </b>25' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-25" title="DD-25">25</a>, <a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-26" title="DD-26">26</a></sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.070</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>Golf Course</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>1 acre</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50%</p>
<p><b>RSA:</b> 50%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS: </b>25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSA, RSX:</b> 30' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>15.30.080</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.335">Government Facility</a></p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>None</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'</p>
<p><b>WD II:</b> 5'/15'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-9" title="DD-9">9</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'</p>
</td>
<td class="center" rowspan="2">
<p>70%</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>70%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>50%</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p><b>RS, WD II, PLA 3C, PLA 6E: </b>25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSA, RSX, PLA 16:</b> 30' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="3">
<p><b>WD II: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-10" title="DD-10">10</a></sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.090</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.528">Mini-School</a> or <a class="def" href="5.10.527">Mini-Day-Care Center</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS, RSX, RSA: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-7" title="DD-7">7</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>12,500 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 6E: </b>3,600 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 16: </b>35,000 sq. ft.</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>5'/15'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-9" title="DD-9">9</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10'</p>
<p><b>PLA 3C:</b> 20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50%</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>50%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 6E: </b>60%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><b>RS, PLA 3C, PLA 6E: </b>25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSX, RSA, PLA 16: </b>30' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.100</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>Piers, Docks, Boat Lifts and Canopies Serving <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Unit</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>None</p>
</td>
<td colspan="3">
<p>See Chapter <a class="cite" href="83" title="Ch. 83&#x2002;Shoreline Management">83</a> KZC.</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>&#x2013;</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>See Chapter <a class="cite" href="83" title="Ch. 83&#x2002;Shoreline Management">83</a> KZC.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>15.30.110</p>
</td>
<td>
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.730">Public Park</a></p>
</td>
<td colspan="6">
<p>Development standards will be determined on a case-by-case basis.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>15.30.120</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.745">Public Utility</a></p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>None</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
<p><b>WD II:</b> 5'/15'<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-9" title="DD-9">9</a></sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center" rowspan="2">
<p>70%</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>70%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>50%</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="2">
<p><b>RS, WD II, PLA 3C, PLA 6E: </b>25' above ABE.</p>
<p><b>RSA, RSX, PLA 16:</b> 30' above ABE.</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="3">
<p><b>WD II: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-10" title="DD-10">10</a></sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="4">
<p>15.30.130</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="4">
<p><a class="def" href="5.10.824">School</a> or <a class="def" href="5.10.192">Day-Care Center</a></p>
</td>
<td rowspan="4">
<p><b>RS, RSX, RSA: </b><sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-7" title="DD-7">7</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>12,500 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 6E: </b>7,200 sq. ft.</p>
<p><b>PLA 16:</b> 35,000 sq. ft.</p>
</td>
<td colspan="3">
<p class="heading">If this use can accommodate 50 or more students or children, then:</p>
</td>
<td class="center" rowspan="4">
<p>70%</p>
<p><b>RSA: </b>70%<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>50%</p>
</td>
<td rowspan="4">
<p><b>RS: </b>25' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-31" title="DD-31">31</a></sup></p>
<p><b>RSX, RSA, PLA 16: </b>30' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-31" title="DD-31">31</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 3C: </b>25' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-6" title="DD-6">6</a></sup></p>
<p><b>PLA 6E: </b>25' above ABE.<sup><a class="cite" href="15.30-DD-31" title="DD-31">31</a></sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>50'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>50'</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="3">
<p class="heading">If this use can accommodate 13 to 49 students or children, then:</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20'</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<p class="heading"><b>Density/Dimensions (DD) Special Regulations:</b></p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-1" data-cite="DD-1"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-1.&#x2002;</span></span>In the WD II zone, for shoreline setbacks see Chapter <a class="cite" href="83" title="Ch. 83&#x2002;Shoreline Management">83</a> KZC.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-2" data-cite="DD-2"><p class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-2.&#x2002;</span></p>


<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-2a" data-cite="DD-2a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Maximum <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling units</a> per acre is six <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling units</a>. Not more than one <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> may be on each <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> regardless of the size of the <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-2b" data-cite="DD-2b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Within a subdivision or short plat the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 5,000 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-2c" data-cite="DD-2c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Road <a class="def" href="5.10.195">dedication</a> and <a class="def" href="5.10.973">vehicular access easements</a> or tracts may not be included in the density calculation or in the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> per <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a>.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-3" data-cite="DD-3"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-3.&#x2002;</span></span>For <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> containing less than 7,200 square feet, the <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">floor area ratio</a> (<a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a>) requirements of KZC <a class="cite" href="115.42" title="115.42&#x2002;Floor Area Ratio (F.A.R.) Calculation for Dwelling Units in Low Density Residential Zones and Attached Dwelling Units in PLA 3C">115.42</a> shall apply. The maximum <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">floor area ratio</a> is 50 percent of the <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>; provided, that <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> may be increased to 60 percent if, with the exception of <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessory</a> features, all roof forms consist of ridgelines peaked near the center of the <a class="def" href="5.10.910">structure</a>, with a minimum pitch of four feet vertical to 12 feet horizontal.</p>



<p>See KZC <a class="cite" href="115.42" title="115.42&#x2002;Floor Area Ratio (F.A.R.) Calculation for Dwelling Units in Low Density Residential Zones and Attached Dwelling Units in PLA 3C">115.42</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">Floor Area Ratio</a> (<a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a>) Calculation for <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Units</a> in Low Density <a class="def" href="5.10.785">Residential Zones</a>, for additional information.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-4" data-cite="DD-4"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-4.&#x2002;</span></span>On corner <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> with two required front yards, one may be reduced to the average of the front yards for the two <a class="def" href="5.10.020">adjoining</a> properties fronting the same <a class="def" href="5.10.900">street</a> as the front yard to be reduced. The <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> may select which front yard will be reduced (see Plate <a class="cite" href="Plate24" title="Plate 24&#x2002;Required Front Yard Averaging">24</a>).</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-5" data-cite="DD-5"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-5.&#x2002;</span></span>The side or rear yard may be reduced to zero feet if the side or rear of the <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> is attached to a <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> on an <a class="def" href="5.10.020">adjoining</a> <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> within the short plat or subdivision.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-6" data-cite="DD-6"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-6.&#x2002;</span></span>Except 30 percent for RSA 1 zone. See RSA General Regulation 1 (KZC <a class="cite" href="15.10.030(1)" title="15.10.030(1)">15.10.030(1)</a>) and KZC <a class="cite" href="15.05.020(3)" title="15.05.020(3)">15.05.020(3)</a>.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-7" data-cite="DD-7"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-7.&#x2002;</span></span>As established on the <a class="def" href="5.10.1000">Zoning Map</a>. Minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7a" data-cite="DD-7a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 35 and RSX 35 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 35,000 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7b" data-cite="DD-7b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 12.5 and RSX 12.5 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 12,500 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7c" data-cite="DD-7c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 8.5 and RSX 8.5 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 8,500 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7d" data-cite="DD-7d"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 7.2 and RSX 7.2 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 7,200 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7e" data-cite="DD-7e"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 6.3 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 6,300 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7f" data-cite="DD-7f"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">f.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 5.0 and RSX 5.0 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 5,000 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7g" data-cite="DD-7g"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">g.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 1 zones, newly platted <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> shall be clustered and configured in a manner to provide generally equal sized <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> outside of the required <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> area.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7h" data-cite="DD-7h"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">h.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 4 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 7,600 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7i" data-cite="DD-7i"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">i.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 6 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 5,100 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-7j" data-cite="DD-7j"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">j.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 8 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 3,800 square feet.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-8" data-cite="DD-8"><p class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-8.&#x2002;</span></p>


<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-8a" data-cite="DD-8a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.910">Structures</a> exceeding 25 feet above <a class="def" href="5.10.045">average building elevation</a> must have the <a class="def" href="5.10.345">ground floor</a> placed below <a class="def" href="5.10.297">existing grade</a> to the extent possible and screened by a vegetative earthen berm.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-8b" data-cite="DD-8b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.910">Structures</a> can be placed at <a class="def" href="5.10.297">existing grade</a> if the <a class="def" href="5.10.910">structures</a> are located on lower ground than adjacent properties and if the adjacent properties are developed and do not contain <a class="def" href="5.10.780">residential use</a>.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-9" data-cite="DD-9"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-9.&#x2002;</span></span>Five feet, but two side yards must equal at least 15 feet.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-10" data-cite="DD-10"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-10.&#x2002;</span></span>The dimension of any <a class="def" href="5.10.775">required yard</a>, other than as specifically listed, will be determined on a case-by-case basis. The City will use the setback for this use in RS zones as a guide.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-11" data-cite="DD-11"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-11.&#x2002;</span></span>As established on the <a class="def" href="5.10.1000">Zoning Map</a>. Minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> per <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> is as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11a" data-cite="DD-11a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 35 and RSX 35 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 35,000 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11b" data-cite="DD-11b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 12.5 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 12,500 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11c" data-cite="DD-11c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 8.5 and RSX 8.5 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 8,500 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11d" data-cite="DD-11d"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 7.2 and RSX 7.2 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 7,200 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11e" data-cite="DD-11e"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 6.3 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 6,300 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11f" data-cite="DD-11f"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">f.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 5.0 and RSX 5.0 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 5,000 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11g" data-cite="DD-11g"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">g.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 35, RSX 35, RS 12.5, RS 8.5, RSX 8.5, RS 7.2, RSX 7.2, RS 6.3, RS 5.0 and RSX 5.0 zones, not more than one <a class="def" href="5.10.250">dwelling unit</a> may be on each <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a>, regardless of the size of each <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11h" data-cite="DD-11h"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">h.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 1 zones, newly platted <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> shall be clustered and configured in a manner to provide generally equal sized <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> outside of the required <a class="def" href="5.10.610">open space</a> area.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11i" data-cite="DD-11i"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">i.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 4 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 7,600 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11j" data-cite="DD-11j"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">j.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 6 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 5,100 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-11k" data-cite="DD-11k"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">k.&#x2002;</span></span>In RSA 8 zones, the minimum <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> is 3,800 square feet.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-12" data-cite="DD-12"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-12.&#x2002;</span></span><a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">Floor Area Ratio</a> (<a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a>) allowed for the <a class="def" href="5.10.920">subject property</a> is as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-12a" data-cite="DD-12a"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 35 and RSX 35 zones, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> is 20 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-12b" data-cite="DD-12b"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 12.5 and RSX 12.5 zones, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> is 35 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-12c" data-cite="DD-12c"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 8.5 and RSX 8.5 zones, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> is 50 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-12d" data-cite="DD-12d"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 7.2 and RSX 7.2 zones, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> is 50 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-12e" data-cite="DD-12e"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 6.3 zones, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> is 50 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-UseSubsec2" id="15.30-DD-12f" data-cite="DD-12f"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">f.&#x2002;</span></span>In RS 5.0 and RSX 5.0 zones, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> is 50 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a>; provided, that <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> may be increased up to 60 percent of <a class="def" href="5.10.482">lot size</a> for the first 5,000 square feet of <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lot</a> area if the following criteria are met:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-UseSubsec3" id="15.30-DD-12f1" data-cite="DD-12f1"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1)&#x2002;</span></span>With the exception of <a class="def" href="5.10.015">accessory</a> features, all roof forms consist of ridgelines peaked near the center of the <a class="def" href="5.10.910">structure</a>, with a minimum pitch of four feet vertical to 12 feet horizontal; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-UseSubsec3" id="15.30-DD-12f2" data-cite="DD-12f2"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2)&#x2002;</span></span>A setback of at least 7.5 feet is provided along each side yard.</p>



<p>See KZC <a class="cite" href="115.42" title="115.42&#x2002;Floor Area Ratio (F.A.R.) Calculation for Dwelling Units in Low Density Residential Zones and Attached Dwelling Units in PLA 3C">115.42</a>, <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">Floor Area Ratio</a> (<a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a>) Calculation for <a class="def" href="5.10.260">Detached Dwelling Units</a> in Low Density <a class="def" href="5.10.785">Residential Zones</a>, for additional information.</p>
</section>
<p>A reduced <a class="def" href="5.10.325.5">F.A.R.</a> may be required pursuant to subdivision design requirements in Chapter <a class="cite ctx-KMC" href="/KMC/22.28" title="Ch. 22.28&#x2002;Design Requirements">22.28</a> KMC.</p>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-13" data-cite="DD-13"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-13.&#x2002;</span></span>Residential <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> in the RS 35, RSX 35 and PLA 16 zones within the Bridle Trails neighborhood north and northeast of Bridle Trails State Park must contain a minimum area of 10,000 permeable square feet, and shall comply with regulations for horses in KZC <a class="cite" href="115.20(5)" title="115.20(5)&#x2002;Horses">115.20(5)</a>.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-14" data-cite="DD-14"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-14.&#x2002;</span></span>On corner <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> with two required front yards, one may be reduced to the average of the front yards for the two <a class="def" href="5.10.020">adjoining</a> properties fronting the same <a class="def" href="5.10.900">street</a> as the front yard to be reduced. The <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> may select which front yard will be reduced (see Plate <a class="cite" href="Plate24" title="Plate 24&#x2002;Required Front Yard Averaging">24</a>).</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-15" data-cite="DD-15"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-15.&#x2002;</span></span>On corner <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a>, only one front yard must be a minimum of 20 feet. All other front yards shall be regulated as a side yard (minimum five-foot yard). The <a class="def" href="5.10.040">applicant</a> may select which front yard shall meet the 20-foot requirement.</p>


</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-DDSubsec1" id="15.30-DD-16" data-cite="DD-16"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">DD-16.&#x2002;</span></span>On <a class="def" href="5.10.480">lots</a> with two front yards that are essentially parallel to one another, only one front yard must be a minimum of 20 feet.
